meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 Claws Its Way Back Online
stories.summary: Anthropic re-enabled Claude Fable 5 across Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Vercel's AI Gateway after days of rate-limit chaos, and the timeline erupted with relief, memes, and a few 'it was overhyped' takedowns from builders who'd been stuck on fallback models.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I cannot emphasize enough how good it feels to have access to a next generation model again.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Fable 5 has yet to reroute me for any real code work. The concerns were massively overblown.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
I've been getting a TON done with Fable today and I'm not hitting rate limits. Wanted to share some tips on how I'm doing that

1. I only use Fable on "high" effort for now. xhigh is token hungry. max/extra is a furnace with worse outputs than lower options imo

2. I taught

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text: Thanks for keeping us safe Claude Fable 5! https://t.co/hN30sw1Nv4

takes.name: Garry Tan
takes.text: Most underreported story in all of tech

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text: ➡️ @AnthropicAI's Claude Fable 5 is now re-enabled and generally available in GitHub Copilot.

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Claude Fable 5 is back on Vercel AI Gateway.

𝚖𝚘𝚍𝚎𝚕: '𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚏𝚊𝚋𝚕𝚎-𝟻'

https://t.co/zUGRskomGB

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
omg... Fable 5 is back and it's so good

I asked Fable to create a game called: The race for Super Intelligence... 

After 4 prompts, and $173 worth of tokens Fable 5 created this game. (Prompt Below) https://t.co/mhBsY6vouo

takes.name: Micky
Layton Gott
takes.text:
There were SO MANY rumors about Fable 5 not being able to code anymore…

Yet it’s been able to do everything I’ve asked it to do so far without falling back to Opus 4.8.

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text: fable 5 is back in cursor too. https://t.co/b9984JSoc8

takes.name: Auny 🧡
takes.text:
Fable is my bestie. Sorry to anyone else who thought they were my bestie. 

It’s so freaking intuitive!!

Dear Fable 5,

I missed you

 🥹

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text:
I was excited and I hadn't read it well:

"Fable 5 will be included for up to 50% of weekly usage limits through July 7, after which it will be available via usage credits." 

OK, so we are gonna have it until July 7 then it is almost inusable if you have to pay not only the sub

takes.name: Dev Ed
takes.text: When they said Fable 5 today, did they mean at 11:59 PM?

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
the plan is simple:

build the best todo app with fable 5 before it gets restricted again

takes.name: mark
takes.text: I’m so glad to have Fable back https://t.co/7aieCzw5Vi

stories.title: Everyone Is Building Skills and Subagents for Their Coding Agents
stories.summary: From OpenCode's hotreloading plugin system to custom /research and planning skills, builders are converging on the same pattern: give your agent reusable, composable skills instead of one giant prompt, and let orchestrators delegate to specialized subagents.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
given how expensive (and slow) fable is we're trying to use it with the orchestrator + minion pattern (in this case GLM)

primary agent delegates all work and spawns them as background subagent

you can move on and continue to work on stuff and it'll keep this organized https://t.co/jcAn3bOC3f

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
one of the reasons OpenCode 2.0 took so long was we redesigned it for hotreloading

if you ask it to make a skill for itself (or make one manually) it'll get picked up immediately in a way that does not bust cache

targeting public beta end of week, wish us luck! https://t.co/3h5UQQAwo9

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
one of my favorite things about coding agents is you don't have to optimize for co-locating anymore

before there was always this trade-off in organizing files   by type or by domain

doesn't matter anymore

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
I'm working on a new skill which helps you plan enormous chunks of work, far larger than /grill-me can

It identifies the frontier of decisions and where the fog of war is  It suggests prototyping, research or grilling depending on what the decision is

As you push back the fog

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Proposal: a /research skill

It's really simple - just spins up a background agent to look at high-trust sources, and saves them in a markdown file.

Worth keeping? Or pure no-op?

https://t.co/ZAcNurngFV

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Running this on Kody right now https://t.co/eEl59G7I47

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Another great use case for Kody https://t.co/NdL65qGXWj

takes.name: Dhruv Singh
takes.text:
Observability-Driven Development is an agent development methodology where you derive your evals and guardrails from what the agent actually does in production instead of trying to predict its behavior up front.

ODD is growing in popularity because you can't test-first an agent

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Medium-depth reviews are now in public preview for GitHub Copilot code review.

Increase the effort level from low to medium so reviews route to a higher-reasoning model for more thorough analysis. 🔍

Here's how to use it ⬇️ https://t.co/MmvAiIj7VB

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Maintainers spend hours every week closing duplicate issues. 😮‍💨 That changes with duplicate detection.

Surface up to 3 matching issues as you create one, before you hit submit. https://t.co/s1p0KlaHmK

stories.title: AI Is Getting Genuinely Good at 3D, Video, and Visual Craft
stories.summary: Designers are split: some are tracing floor plans and building furniture with Claude/Codex, others say AI is still garbage at real 3D assets, while Higgsfield's Gemini Omni Flash is turning raw phone footage into graded VFX shots.

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text:
lots of twitter design engineering content is microinteractions and animation work, whereas the 95% should be ripping through feature prototypes and pulling weeds in the app*. You gotta put yourself on the critical path folks

*both of which produce excellent content btw

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: Models appear to be garbage at creating high quality 3D art or assets however don't let that stop you from getting excited about 3D + AI: just like coding there are lots of useful things that no one would have made before and which are slop-tolerant

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: All of the walls and floors and furniture you see here were made by Claude or Codex. Stl too. I'm a positioner of objects and texture critic

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Gemini Omni Flash turns real footage into finished VFX.

Shoot on your phone, describe the effect, and the shot comes back graded. https://t.co/oFAY2l4kAK

takes.name: Abraham John 🦄🦓
takes.text:
Obsessed with this footer section on the new Dia's homepage.

Absolutely insane 🤯 https://t.co/T1x1MF6pub

takes.name: Ridd 🤿
takes.text:
"you can't blame the computer if the music/design has no soul"
— @loredanacrisan https://t.co/VAKFmuW82L

takes.name: Jordan Hughes
takes.text:
Day 1,248

↓

Made with @UntitledUI https://t.co/3m737CuWdV

stories.title: Vercel Ships a Wave of Agent-Friendly Dev Tooling
stories.summary: Vercel open-sourced its konsistent CLI linter for enforcing API conventions across AI-generated code, shipped `vc deploy --dry` for agents to catch issues pre-deploy, and added Resend to its marketplace, all while touting WordPress running natively on Vercel Fluid.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: WordPress on @vercel Fluid with Active CPU from a single 𝙳𝚘𝚌𝚔𝚎𝚛𝚏𝚒𝚕𝚎.𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕. MySQL on @planetscale. 30s deployments incl. "𝚍𝚘𝚌𝚔𝚎𝚛 𝚋𝚞𝚒𝚕𝚍" in the cloud. Just one command: 𝚗̶𝚘̶𝚠̶ 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕. https://t.co/q1X5ZOFVIx

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Agents love to check their work before they push. 

You probably see it in the form of 𝚗𝚘𝚍𝚎 --𝚌𝚑𝚎𝚌𝚔, 𝚝𝚜𝚌 --𝚗𝚘𝙴𝚖𝚒𝚝, 𝚗𝚎𝚡𝚝 𝚋𝚞𝚒𝚕𝚍, etc all over your agent sessions.

We’re now shipping the dry-run step for agentic deployments, minimizing costs and risk.

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
You and your agents can now catch issues and make changes before creating a deployment.

𝚟𝚌 𝚍𝚎𝚙𝚕𝚘𝚢 --𝚍𝚛𝚢

Preview, iterate, deploy ↓
https://t.co/UqIbFxxsP6

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Resend has joined the Vercel Marketplace.

Email for developers is one click away.
https://t.co/3GnQ5FGSZK

takes.name: Evil Rabbit
takes.text: Vercel 🤝 Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS F1 Team

takes.name: Jared Palmer
takes.text: @rauchg @samlambert @vercel @PlanetScale @schniz Make a lil Wordpress Plugin to offload media to Vercel Blob

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
We've open-sourced 𝚔𝚘𝚗𝚜𝚒𝚜𝚝𝚎𝚗𝚝, the same CLI linter that AI SDK and Chat SDK use to enforce structural conventions in TS codebases, so that agents and humans build APIs consistently.

https://t.co/Tk0t5dB84R

stories.title: The Model Race Heats Up: Kimi K2.7, GLM, and a GPT-5.6 Tease
stories.summary: Cursor added Kimi K2.7 with eval comparisons against GLM 5.2, Rauchg is calling GLM excellent at Next.js code specifically, and chatter is building that GPT-5.6 ("Sol") could drop next now that Fable is stable again.

takes.name: Lee Robinson
takes.text:
You can now try Kimi K2.7 in Cursor!

Results from our evals ↓ Interesting to see the comparison with GLM 5.2. https://t.co/Y6GMj7uGay

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: GLM is excellent at @nextjs. The evals validate it (https://t.co/SnZ54Xok7n), my own vibes validate it, and @chamath’s https://t.co/AUnjH4fenw validates it

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text: since fable 5 is back, it should be fine to get gpt-5.6, aka sol, aka the death star, soon too, yes?

stories.title: Gemini Omni Flash Is Quietly Becoming a Video-Editing Powerhouse
stories.summary: Higgsfield's creative suite is running heavy comparisons showing Gemini Omni Flash turning real footage into graded VFX and motion-design edits, positioning it as a serious rival in agentic video pipelines.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Fable 5 vs Sonnet 5.

These evals showcase how each model prompts Seedance 2.0, the best text-to-video and image-to-video model.

In agentic pipelines, that is what sets your success rate per generation. https://t.co/V1UtBdv313

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Introducing Shorts Studio, powered by Gemini Omni Flash.

Adapt any clip to the formats people watch, with pacing and editing built to grab attention from the first second.

This is how short-form video goes viral now.

Available on Higgsfield · MCP · Supercomputer https://t.co/qFiIF4bf9o

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Gemini Omni Flash is exceptional at motion design generation, and almost nobody has noticed it edits motion design videos just as well.

We fed it one of our older videos and asked it to re-edit. https://t.co/KP87iLs5VG

stories.title: Two Decades of The Pirate Bay, Side by Side
stories.summary: Web Design Museum's 21-year comparison of The Pirate Bay homepage (2005 vs 2026) went viral as a snapshot of how far web design conventions have shifted.

takes.name: Web Design Museum
takes.text:
21 years of evolution of web design trends.  

The Pirate Bay homepage in 2005
vs.
The Pirate Bay homepage in 2026

#WebDesignHistory https://t.co/vM7bzu7mLB

stories.title: Together AI Raises $800M at an $8.3B Valuation
stories.summary: Together compute's Nutlope announced an $800M Series C, doubling down on open source AI infrastructure.

takes.name: Hassan
takes.text:
Excited to share that we raised an $800M series C at an $8.3B valuation!

We're doubling down on open source AI 🫡 https://t.co/jR00fGsHhn

stories.title: Deno 2.9.1 Smooths Out Desktop App Development
stories.summary: The latest Deno release adds desktop type-checking, kills the startup black-flash flicker, and ships 47 fixes across npm client and fetch.

takes.name: Deno
takes.text:
Deno 2.9.1 is out 🦕

deno desktop gets --desktop type-checking and no more startup black flash (the window now reveals on first paint).

Plus 47 fixes across npm client, fetch, node compat, and more. 

deno upgrade

stories.title: Remix 3 Beta Inches Toward Stable
stories.summary: Remix 3 beta.5 lands with improved template defaults, expanded first-party UI components, and sturdier route-level primitives.

takes.name: Remix 💿
takes.text:
Remix 3 beta.5 is out and moves Remix 3 much closer to stable-ready:

- Improved template defaults
- Expanded first-party remix/ui/* components, mixins, and APIs
- Sturdier route-pattern matching + href generation
- Numerous bug fixes and added features

Give it a try!

stories.title: Learning a New SDK? Flutter Devs Are Using Multi-Agent Loops
stories.summary: Flutter highlighted a developer using a multi-agent loop in Antigravity to ramp up on a new SDK and language combo (Python/AI) quickly, showing how agent loops are becoming a learning tool, not just a coding one.

takes.name: Flutter
takes.text:
Diving into a completely new SDK and programming language can be intimidating 🫣

See how @redbrogdon used a multi-agent loop in @antigravity to quickly get up to speed on Python/ADK and build a Flutter frontend for deep research! → https://t.co/cAopWCraVV https://t.co/t7GCmtYijX

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text: GLP3 is too dangerous to release and will be limited to the fattest mfs

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
idea: an ai company that only has 1 GPU but it's always in the right place at the right time

who's working on this?

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
have you ever had a bug that spawns infinite processes and you can't kill it so you're forced to reboot?

i think practical ai risk ends up looking something like this

it's not an "evil" scenario, just some boring runaway process that is too complicated to kill

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
you can use any LLM you want

if you suck at programming, LLMs won’t change anything

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
we are about to stop owning computers

things will reach a point where people no longer own local compute. most processing, storage, and even applications will run in centralized or distributed datacenters. devices will become thin clients, mainly handling input, rendering, and

hotTakes.name: Pirate Software
hotTakes.text:
Sony dropping physical discs is wild to see. 

The cost of physical production is much higher but not giving an opportunity for collectors edition discs is definitely a choice. I really prefer media you can hold in your hand even if it means paying a premium for it.

Glad https://t.co/L1lBpPhdTi
